title: Chainspot - Open App
meta_description: chainspot routes wallets to cross-chain swaps, bridge paths, and DeFi access across EVM and non-EVM networks. Open the official app.
hero_h2: Cross-chain swaps and bridge routes from one app
hero_lead: Chainspot routes wallets into an omnichain DeFi app for cross-chain swaps, bridge paths, liquidity routing, and EVM plus non-EVM access.
what_is: Chainspot is a non-custodian omnichain DeFi protocol for managing DeFi positions across EVM and non-EVM networks, as described in its official documentation. The app focuses on cross-chain swaps, bridging, and routing through integrated liquidity providers.

how.d: Use the app domain, confirm the URL, and start from the wallet connection screen.
Choose the wallet and network you want to send from before selecting the destination chain.
Pick the token, source network, destination network, and amount so the router can show available paths.
Check output, slippage, fees, destination address, and route details before signing in your wallet.

points.p: The app prepares routes through integrated DEXes, bridges, and routers while users review and sign from their own wallet.
Official ecosystem docs list Ethereum, Polygon, BNB Chain, Arbitrum, Avalanche, Tron, TON, and other supported networks; check the ecosystem page before routing.
The security docs describe audited smart contracts, non-custodial design, and off-chain modules used for routing and analytics rather than fund custody.

faq.a: chainspot is an omnichain DeFi protocol and app for wallet-based cross-chain swaps, bridge routing, and DeFi position access across EVM and non-EVM networks.
This landing page is not the app; it routes users to app.chainspot.io, which is the official dApp domain shown by Chainspot.
The official docs describe Chainspot as non-custodial, with users signing transactions from their own wallets. Always review the transaction before approving it.
The ecosystem documentation lists networks including Ethereum, Polygon, BNB Chain, Arbitrum One, Avalanche, Tron, TON, and more; confirm current availability inside the app.

faq.q: Can chainspot show different bridge routes?
faq.a: Yes, Chainspot uses liquidity routing through integrated providers, so users should compare route details, quoted output, slippage, fees, and destination address before signing.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify app.chainspot.io before connecting a wallet. Educational only, not financial advice.
